div1.entryFree:
Digress, 1) to deviate, to swerve: I am come to keep my word, though in some part enforced to d. Shr. III, 2, 109. --ing from the valour of a man, Rom. III, 3, 127.
2) to transgress, to offend: thy --ing son, R2 V, 3, 66. I do d. too much, citing my worthless praise, Tit. V, 3, 116.
